# QaaS Documentation

> Runner, Mocker, Framework, hook catalog, and offline packaging documentation.

This index is generated from checked frontmatter summaries. Use the full-content shards when page bodies are needed.

## Tutorials

- [Create a Mock (YAML)](/mocker/quickStart/createMock/): Use YAML to define mock data sources, stubs, and servers in a declarative mock configuration.
- [Create a Mock (Code)](/mocker/quickStart/createMockCode/): This sample builds the same mock as the YAML quick start, but assembles the mock configuration in Program.cs.
- [Deploy a Mock](/mocker/quickStart/deployMock/): Once the mock works locally, package it as a container image and deploy it with a native Helm chart.
- [Mocker IDE Setup](/mocker/quickStart/ide/): QaaS.Mocker can be configured in YAML or in C# (Configuration as Code). This page focuses on the YAML experience for Mocker projects.
- [Installation](/mocker/quickStart/installation/): Add the relevant package source to your global NuGet.Config file, usually ~/.nuget/NuGet/NuGet.Config.
- [Integrate with Tests](/mocker/quickStart/integrateWithTests/): For end-to-end integration between QaaS.Runner tests and a mock server, configure the Controller section in mocker.qaas.yaml.
- [Debug Test Failure](/qaas/quickStart/debugTestFailure/): Use split Runner phases, focused logging, and saved report artifacts to debug failed tests.
- [Runner IDE Setup](/qaas/quickStart/ide/): QaaS.Runner can be configured in YAML or in C# (Configuration as Code). This page focuses on the YAML experience for Runner projects.
- [Installation](/qaas/quickStart/installation/): Add the relevant package source to your global NuGet.Config file, usually ~/.nuget/NuGet/NuGet.Config.
- [Maintainable Test](/qaas/quickStart/makeTestMoreMaintainable/): Use anchors, placeholders, overwrite files, and cases to keep Runner YAML reusable across environments and variants.
- [Run Test](/qaas/quickStart/runTest/): Once the local quick-start environment is ready, run the sample from the project directory.
- [Writing Custom Hooks in QaaS for Advanced Testing](/qaas/quickStart/writeHooks/): Implement custom generators, processors, probes, or assertions when packaged hooks do not cover the required behavior.
- [Write a Test (Code)](/qaas/quickStart/writeTestCode/): Build the Runner quick-start configuration directly in `Program.cs` when C# should own the test setup.
- [Write a Test (YAML)](/qaas/quickStart/writeTestYaml/): Define DataSources, Sessions, and Assertions in YAML when the test flow should stay declarative.

## How-To

- [Custom Assertions — Authoring Guide](/assertions/custom-authoring-guide/): Derive a custom assertion from BaseAssertion<TConfig> in QaaS.Framework.SDK and wire it into an Assertions[] entry.
- [Casting & Serializing Data — Quick Guide](/framework/functions/casting-and-serialization/): Use the QaasSerializer facade and the typed casting extension methods to turn raw session payloads into typed objects in one call.
- [Custom Generators — Authoring Guide](/generators/custom-authoring-guide/): Derive a custom generator from BaseGenerator<TConfig> in QaaS.Framework.SDK and wire it into a DataSource.
- [Runner + Mocker Controller (Redis) Recipe](/mocker/authoring-guide/controller-recipe-redis/): End-to-end Runner + Mocker recipe wired through a Redis-backed Controller: readiness probe, channel naming, ack/timeout behavior, and a full MockerCommands block.
- [Mocker — Multi-Server Routing](/mocker/authoring-guide/multi-server-routing/): Expose multiple named HTTP servers from a single mocker process so a runner can target each one deterministically by name and instance.
- [Custom Probes — Authoring Guide](/probes/custom-authoring-guide/): Derive a custom probe from BaseProbe<TConfig> in QaaS.Framework.SDK and wire it into a session via the Probes block.
- [Custom Processors — Authoring Guide](/processors/custom-authoring-guide/): Derive a custom transaction processor from BaseTransactionProcessor<TConfig> and wire it into a mocker stub.
- [Action Selection Playbook](/qaas/quickStart/actionSelectionPlaybook/): Choose Runner action types, name evidence consistently, and combine Runner with Mocker for repeatable tests.
- [Hello-World HTTP Test](/qaas/quickStart/helloWorldHttp/): Smallest end-to-end QaaS recipe: a runner test against a mocker stub that returns HTTP 200 on /hello, with act/assert replay for fast iteration.
- [S3 headers and key selection](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/s3-headers-and-key-selection/): Publish S3 user metadata, consume it for assertions, and select one key hierarchy level with Prefix and Delimiter.
- [Runner — YAML Schema Extensions](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/schema-extensions/): Register a new plugin family with QaaS.JsonSchemaExtensions by tagging the base with [JsonSchema] and emitting AnyOfSchemaOption discriminators, then regenerate the runner schema.

## Explanation

- [Assertions](/assertions/): Assertions validate Runner session data after the act phase and turn observed inputs and outputs into reportable results.
- [Framework Functions](/framework/functions/): This overview indexes the current public function surface documented through source-level qaas-docs annotations and extension-method XML docs.
- [QaaS.Framework](/framework/): QaaS.Framework is the shared runtime and SDK layer used by Runner, Mocker, and custom hook packages.
- [Generators](/generators/): Generators produce DataSources that Runner sessions, assertions, and chained generators can consume.
- [QaaS Documentation](/): QaaS docs cover Runner tests, Mocker services, Framework APIs, shared hooks, integrations, and offline packaging from the same MkDocs source.
- [Architecture](/mocker/architecture/): QaaS.Mocker loads configuration into DataSources, Stubs, Servers, and an optional Controller before serving mock traffic.
- [Mocker Functions](/mocker/functions/): This overview indexes the current public function surface documented through source-level qaas-docs annotations and extension-method XML docs.
- [QaaS Mocker](/mocker/): QaaS.Mocker runs HTTP, gRPC, and socket mocks from YAML or C# configuration and routes traffic to named stubs.
- [Controller](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/controller/overview/): The Controller section lets Runner Mocker Commands coordinate a running mock through Redis-backed control channels.
- [DataSources](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/dataSources/overview/): The list of the data sources that contain enumerables of data to be generated and iterated later in several places.
- [Servers](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/server/overview/): Servers defines the HTTP, gRPC, or socket endpoints that QaaS.Mocker starts for a mock execution.
- [Stubs](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/stubs/overview/): Stubs map named mock actions to transaction processor hooks and optional data-source inputs.
- [Probes](/probes/): Probes perform setup, cleanup, and infrastructure actions before or after Runner sessions.
- [Processors](/processors/): Processors are hooks that create mock responses in QaaS.Mocker. They implement BaseTransactionProcessor<TConfig> and are referenced by class name from a Stub.
- [Architecture](/qaas/architecture/): QaaS.Framework runtime is built around a Plugins architecture that extends and customizes QaaS by integrating user-provided packages.
- [Runner Functions](/qaas/functions/): This overview indexes the current public function surface documented through source-level qaas-docs annotations and extension-method XML docs.
- [QaaS Runner](/qaas/): QaaS.Runner loads YAML or C# test configuration, runs sessions, evaluates assertions, and writes report output.
- [Assertions](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/assertions/overview/): Assertions validate Runner session data after act-phase work and write assertion results to Allure output.
- [DataSources](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/dataSources/overview/): The list of data sources that contain enumerables of data to be used for the rest of the test.
- [Links](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/links/overview/): Links add per-result observability URLs to Runner reports, including Kibana, Prometheus, and Grafana links.
- [MetaData](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/metaData/overview/): Define test ownership, system identity, and optional labels for Runner reports and filtering.
- [Reporters](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/reporters/overview/): Set run-level reporter defaults for Allure artifacts and optional ReportPortal publishing.
- [Sessions](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/overview/): Sessions define the runtime actions Runner performs against the system under test and supporting infrastructure.
- [Storages](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/storages/overview/): Storages define where Runner writes and reads persisted runtime objects for act and assert commands.

## Reference

- [DelayByAverage Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/DelayByAverage/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for DelayByAverage AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [DelayByAverage Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/DelayByAverage/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for DelayByAverage AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [DelayByAverage](/assertions/availableAssertions/DelayByAverage/overview/): Checks for delay between an input source to an output source by subtracting the average timestamp of all inputs from the average timestamp of all the outputs.
- [DelayByChunks Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/DelayByChunks/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for DelayByChunks AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [DelayByChunks Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/DelayByChunks/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for DelayByChunks AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [DelayByChunks](/assertions/availableAssertions/DelayByChunks/overview/): Checks delay between input and output chunks using configured chunk sizes and timestamp differences.
- [HermeticByExpectedOutputCount Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByExpectedOutputCount/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for HermeticByExpectedOutputCount AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [HermeticByExpectedOutputCount Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByExpectedOutputCount/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for HermeticByExpectedOutputCount AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [HermeticByExpectedOutputCount](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByExpectedOutputCount/overview/): Performs a hermetic test by comparing the count of a given output in a session to a given expected count
- [HermeticByExpectedOutputCountInRange Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByExpectedOutputCountInRange/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for HermeticByExpectedOutputCountInRange AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [HermeticByExpectedOutputCountInRange Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByExpectedOutputCountInRange/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for HermeticByExpectedOutputCountInRange AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [HermeticByExpectedOutputCountInRange](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByExpectedOutputCountInRange/overview/): Performs a hermetic test by comparing the count of a given output in a session to a given expected minimum and maximum
- [HermeticByInputOutputPercentage Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByInputOutputPercentage/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for HermeticByInputOutputPercentage AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [HermeticByInputOutputPercentage Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByInputOutputPercentage/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for HermeticByInputOutputPercentage AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [HermeticByInputOutputPercentage](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByInputOutputPercentage/overview/): Performs a hermetic test by comparing the the count of a specified input with multiplied by given percentage modifier to the count of a specified output
- [HermeticByInputOutputPercentageInRange Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByInputOutputPercentageInRange/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for HermeticByInputOutputPercentageInRange AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [HermeticByInputOutputPercentageInRange Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByInputOutputPercentageInRange/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for HermeticByInputOutputPercentageInRange AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [HermeticByInputOutputPercentageInRange](/assertions/availableAssertions/HermeticByInputOutputPercentageInRange/overview/): Checks whether the percentage between configured inputs and outputs stays within the expected minimum and maximum range.
- [HttpStatus Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HttpStatus/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for HttpStatus AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [HttpStatus Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/HttpStatus/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for HttpStatus AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [HttpStatus](/assertions/availableAssertions/HttpStatus/overview/): Performs a logic test on the http status of all selected outputs in a session by checking they all have the desired http status code
- [ObjectOutputJsonSchema Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/ObjectOutputJsonSchema/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for ObjectOutputJsonSchema AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [ObjectOutputJsonSchema Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/ObjectOutputJsonSchema/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for ObjectOutputJsonSchema AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [ObjectOutputJsonSchema](/assertions/availableAssertions/ObjectOutputJsonSchema/overview/): Validates that each configured output item matches at least one JSON schema provided by the configured data sources.
- [OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults](/assertions/availableAssertions/OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults/overview/): Checks that the configured output content matches the expected values loaded from a CSV results file.
- [OutputDeserializableTo Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/OutputDeserializableTo/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for OutputDeserializableTo AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [OutputDeserializableTo Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/OutputDeserializableTo/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for OutputDeserializableTo AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [OutputDeserializableTo](/assertions/availableAssertions/OutputDeserializableTo/overview/): Performs a logic test that checks if the items of a configured output can all be deserialized using a configured deserializer
- [ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage Configurations Table View](/assertions/availableAssertions/ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage AssertionConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from assertions.schema.json.
- [ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage Configurations Yaml View](/assertions/availableAssertions/ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage AssertionConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage](/assertions/availableAssertions/ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage/overview/): Compares the hermetic percentage calculated from configured inputs and outputs with the hermetic percentage reported by the metrics output.
- [Data Sources: Collection helpers](/framework/functions/builders/data-sources-sections/collection-helpers/): Reference page for Data Sources: Collection helpers.

- [Extension Methods](/framework/functions/extension-methods/): This page collects public Framework extension methods that have XML documentation or explicit docs annotations.
- [Placeholders](/framework/functions/placeholders/): Each entry uses the short function name as the table-of-contents label. Expand an entry to inspect its source file, signature, and XML doc comments.
- [Updates](/framework/functions/updates/): Each entry uses the short function name as the table-of-contents label. Expand an entry to inspect its source file, signature, and XML doc comments.
- [YAML](/framework/functions/yaml/): Each entry uses the short function name as the table-of-contents label. Expand an entry to inspect its source file, signature, and XML doc comments.
- [QaaS.Framework.Configurations](/framework/projects/configuration/): QaaS.Framework.Configurations loads, expands, binds, validates, and updates YAML-backed configuration.
- [QaaS.Framework.Executions](/framework/projects/executions/): QaaS.Framework.Executions provides shared execution infrastructure used by Runner and Mocker.
- [QaaS.Framework.Infrastructure](/framework/projects/infrastructure/): QaaS.Framework.Infrastructure provides shared primitives consumed by the other Framework packages.
- [QaaS.Framework.Policies](/framework/projects/policies/): QaaS.Framework.Policies turns policy configuration objects into runtime policy chains for repeated communication actions.
- [QaaS.Framework.Protocols](/framework/projects/protocols/): QaaS.Framework.Protocols provides external I/O contracts, configurations, implementations, factories, and metadata helpers.
- [QaaS.Framework.Providers](/framework/projects/providers/): QaaS.Framework.Providers discovers hook implementations and resolves configured hook names at runtime.
- [QaaS.Framework.SDK](/framework/projects/sdk/): QaaS.Framework.SDK is the main runtime-contract package. It defines the object surface QaaS works with: contexts, execution data, sessions, communications.
- [QaaS.Framework.Serialization](/framework/projects/serialization/): QaaS.Framework.Serialization provides shared serializers and deserializers used by Framework and SDK packages.
- [FromCSV Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromCSV/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FromCSV GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [FromCSV Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromCSV/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FromCSV GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FromCSV](/generators/availableGenerators/FromCSV/overview/): Reads CSV files from the configured file-system path and turns each row into generated data items.
- [FromDataLake Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromDataLake/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FromDataLake GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [FromDataLake Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromDataLake/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FromDataLake GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FromDataLake](/generators/availableGenerators/FromDataLake/overview/): Retrieves rows from the configured data lake query and exposes each row as a generated JSON object.
- [FromDataSources Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromDataSources/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FromDataSources GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [FromDataSources Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromDataSources/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FromDataSources GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FromDataSources](/generators/availableGenerators/FromDataSources/overview/): Generates data from the enumerable of data sources it receives
- [FromFileSystem Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromFileSystem/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FromFileSystem GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [FromFileSystem Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromFileSystem/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FromFileSystem GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FromFileSystem](/generators/availableGenerators/FromFileSystem/overview/): Retrieves data from files under a configured path in the local file system.
- [FromLettuceDataSources Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromLettuceDataSources/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FromLettuceDataSources GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [FromLettuceDataSources Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromLettuceDataSources/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FromLettuceDataSources GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FromLettuceDataSources](/generators/availableGenerators/FromLettuceDataSources/overview/): Generates data from the enumerable of data sources it receives that is in `Lettuce` file format, presumes all items in the enumerable are deserialized into <see cref="SerializationType.Json"/>
- [FromS3 Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromS3/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FromS3 GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [FromS3 Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromS3/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FromS3 GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FromS3](/generators/availableGenerators/FromS3/overview/): Retrieves data from objects in a configured S3 bucket and prefix.
- [FromSessionDataDataSources Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromSessionDataDataSources/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FromSessionDataDataSources GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [FromSessionDataDataSources Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/FromSessionDataDataSources/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FromSessionDataDataSources GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FromSessionDataDataSources](/generators/availableGenerators/FromSessionDataDataSources/overview/): Generates data from the enumerable of data sources it receives, presumes all items in the enumerable are serialized and can be treated as a byte array
- [Json Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/Json/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for Json GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [Json Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/Json/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for Json GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [Json](/generators/availableGenerators/Json/overview/): Generates JSON data from a configured prototype document.
- [JsonSchemaDraft4 Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/JsonSchemaDraft4/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for JsonSchemaDraft4 GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [JsonSchemaDraft4 Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/JsonSchemaDraft4/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for JsonSchemaDraft4 GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [JsonSchemaDraft4](/generators/availableGenerators/JsonSchemaDraft4/overview/): Generates JSON data that conforms to a configured JSON Schema Draft 4 document.
- [LettuceFromFileSystem Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/LettuceFromFileSystem/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for LettuceFromFileSystem GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [LettuceFromFileSystem Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/LettuceFromFileSystem/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for LettuceFromFileSystem GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [LettuceFromFileSystem](/generators/availableGenerators/LettuceFromFileSystem/overview/): Retrieves lettuce-formatted files from the configured file-system path and exposes them as generated messages with their routing key metadata.
- [Stacking Configurations Table View](/generators/availableGenerators/Stacking/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for Stacking GeneratorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from generators.schema.json.
- [Stacking Configurations Yaml View](/generators/availableGenerators/Stacking/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for Stacking GeneratorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [Stacking](/generators/availableGenerators/Stacking/overview/): Combines multiple data sources by taking a configured number of items from each source in turn.

- [Executions](/mocker/functions/builders/executions/): Each entry uses the short function name as the table-of-contents label. Expand an entry to inspect its source file, signature, and XML doc comments.
- [Transaction Stubs: Collection helpers](/mocker/functions/builders/transaction-stubs-sections/collection-helpers/): Reference page for Transaction Stubs: Collection helpers.

- [Mocker Runner](/mocker/functions/commands/mocker-runner/): Each entry uses the short function name as the table-of-contents label. Expand an entry to inspect its source file, signature, and XML doc comments.
- [Extension Methods: Data](/mocker/functions/extension-methods-sections/data/): Reference page for Extension Methods: Data.
- [Extension Methods: Http](/mocker/functions/extension-methods-sections/http/): Reference page for Extension Methods: Http.
- [Extension Methods: Socket](/mocker/functions/extension-methods-sections/socket/): Reference page for Extension Methods: Socket.
- [Extension Methods](/mocker/functions/extension-methods/): This page collects public Mocker extension methods that have XML documentation or explicit docs annotations.
- [Commands](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/commands/commands/): QaaS Mocker exposes a compact command surface: one command to start the runtime and one command to render the effective configuration without starting it.
- [run](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/commands/run/): Start the configured mock servers and optional controller runtime.
- [template](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/commands/template/): Render the effective merged configuration after file, folder, argument, and environment overrides.
- [Configuration Sections](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/configurationSections/): Each configuration section in the mocker.qaas.yaml file is used to perform a different action and has its own configurations and descriptions.
- [Controller Configurations Table View](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/controller/configurations/tableView/): Controller Configurations Table View
- [Controller Configurations Yaml View](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/controller/configurations/yamlView/): Controller configuration YAML example.
- [DataSources Configurations Table View](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/dataSources/configurations/tableView/): DataSources Configurations Table View
- [DataSources Configurations Yaml View](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/dataSources/configurations/yamlView/): Data source configuration YAML example.
- [Servers Configurations Table View](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/server/configurations/tableView/): Servers Configurations Table View
- [Servers Configurations Yaml View](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/server/configurations/yamlView/): Server configuration YAML example.
- [Stubs Configurations Table View](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/stubs/configurations/tableView/): Stubs Configurations Table View
- [Stubs Configurations Yaml View](/mocker/userInterfaces/mocker/configurationSections/stubs/configurations/yamlView/): Stub configuration YAML example.
- [CreateRabbitMqBindings Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqBindings/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for CreateRabbitMqBindings ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.

- [CreateRabbitMqBindings](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqBindings/overview/): Creates RabbitMQ bindings between exchanges and queues or between exchanges by using the configured binding definitions.
- [CreateRabbitMqExchanges Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqExchanges/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for CreateRabbitMqExchanges ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.

- [CreateRabbitMqExchanges](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqExchanges/overview/): Creates one or more RabbitMQ exchanges with the configured durability, auto-delete, and arguments.
- [CreateRabbitMqQueues Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqQueues/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for CreateRabbitMqQueues ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.

- [CreateRabbitMqQueues](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqQueues/overview/): Creates one or more RabbitMQ queues with the configured queue arguments.
- [CreateRabbitMqUsers Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqUsers/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for CreateRabbitMqUsers ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [CreateRabbitMqUsers Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqUsers/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for CreateRabbitMqUsers ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [CreateRabbitMqUsers](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqUsers/overview/): Creates RabbitMQ users through the management API with the configured credentials and tags.
- [CreateRabbitMqVirtualHosts Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqVirtualHosts/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for CreateRabbitMqVirtualHosts ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [CreateRabbitMqVirtualHosts Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqVirtualHosts/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for CreateRabbitMqVirtualHosts ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [CreateRabbitMqVirtualHosts](/probes/availableProbes/CreateRabbitMqVirtualHosts/overview/): Creates RabbitMQ virtual hosts through the management API so later probes and sessions can use them.
- [CreateS3Bucket Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateS3Bucket/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for CreateS3Bucket ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [CreateS3Bucket Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/CreateS3Bucket/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for CreateS3Bucket ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [CreateS3Bucket](/probes/availableProbes/CreateS3Bucket/overview/): Ensures the configured S3 bucket exists by creating it when it is missing.

- [DeleteElasticIndices](/probes/availableProbes/DeleteElasticIndices/overview/): Deletes every Elasticsearch index that matches the configured index pattern.

- [DeleteS3Bucket Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/DeleteS3Bucket/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for DeleteS3Bucket ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [DeleteS3Bucket](/probes/availableProbes/DeleteS3Bucket/overview/): Empties the configured S3 bucket and deletes it, treating a missing bucket as an already-satisfied state.
- [DownloadRabbitMqDefinitions Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/DownloadRabbitMqDefinitions/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for DownloadRabbitMqDefinitions ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.

- [DownloadRabbitMqDefinitions](/probes/availableProbes/DownloadRabbitMqDefinitions/overview/): Downloads RabbitMQ definitions from the management API so the current topology can be captured and reused.
- [DropMongoDbCollection Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/DropMongoDbCollection/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for DropMongoDbCollection ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.

- [DropMongoDbCollection](/probes/availableProbes/DropMongoDbCollection/overview/): Drops the configured MongoDB collection so a later run can recreate it from scratch.

- [EmptyElasticIndices](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyElasticIndices/overview/): Empties elastic indices by their index pattern
- [EmptyMongoDbCollection Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyMongoDbCollection/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for EmptyMongoDbCollection ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [EmptyMongoDbCollection Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyMongoDbCollection/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for EmptyMongoDbCollection ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [EmptyMongoDbCollection](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyMongoDbCollection/overview/): Deletes all documents from the configured MongoDB collection so it starts clean for the test run.
- [EmptyRedisByChunks Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyRedisByChunks/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for EmptyRedisByChunks ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [EmptyRedisByChunks Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyRedisByChunks/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for EmptyRedisByChunks ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [EmptyRedisByChunks](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyRedisByChunks/overview/): Scans the selected Redis database in batches and deletes matching keys, optionally filtering them by a regular expression.
- [EmptyS3Bucket Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyS3Bucket/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for EmptyS3Bucket ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [EmptyS3Bucket Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyS3Bucket/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for EmptyS3Bucket ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [EmptyS3Bucket](/probes/availableProbes/EmptyS3Bucket/overview/): Deletes objects from the configured S3 bucket, optionally constrained to a prefix, while treating a missing bucket as a no-op.
- [ExecuteRedisCommand Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/ExecuteRedisCommand/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for ExecuteRedisCommand ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [ExecuteRedisCommand Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/ExecuteRedisCommand/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for ExecuteRedisCommand ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [ExecuteRedisCommand](/probes/availableProbes/ExecuteRedisCommand/overview/): Executes one Redis command with optional arguments against the selected Redis database, optionally storing the result for later redisResults placeholder reuse.
- [ExecuteRedisCommands Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/ExecuteRedisCommands/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for ExecuteRedisCommands ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [ExecuteRedisCommands Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/ExecuteRedisCommands/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for ExecuteRedisCommands ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [ExecuteRedisCommands](/probes/availableProbes/ExecuteRedisCommands/overview/): Executes multiple Redis commands sequentially against the selected Redis database, allowing later commands to reuse earlier results through redisResults placeholders and optional looping.
- [FlushAllRedis Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/FlushAllRedis/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FlushAllRedis ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [FlushAllRedis Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/FlushAllRedis/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FlushAllRedis ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FlushAllRedis](/probes/availableProbes/FlushAllRedis/overview/): Runs Redis FLUSHALL against the configured server to remove keys from every database.
- [FlushDbRedis Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/FlushDbRedis/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for FlushDbRedis ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [FlushDbRedis Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/FlushDbRedis/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for FlushDbRedis ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [FlushDbRedis](/probes/availableProbes/FlushDbRedis/overview/): Runs Redis FLUSHDB against the selected Redis database.

- [OsExecuteCommandsInContainers](/probes/availableProbes/OsExecuteCommandsInContainers/overview/): Probe that Executes a command passed by the `cmd` string variable on every pod and container if passed to the function

- [OsRestartPods](/probes/availableProbes/OsRestartPods/overview/): Probe that restarts all pods with configured labels in the configured namespace

- [OsUpdateDeploymentImage](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateDeploymentImage/overview/): Updates the image of one container in a Kubernetes or OpenShift deployment.
- [OsUpdateDeploymentResources Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateDeploymentResources/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for OsUpdateDeploymentResources ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [OsUpdateDeploymentResources Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateDeploymentResources/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for OsUpdateDeploymentResources ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [OsUpdateDeploymentResources](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateDeploymentResources/overview/): Updates container resource requests and limits in a Kubernetes or OpenShift deployment.
- [OsUpdateStatefulSetImage Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateStatefulSetImage/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for OsUpdateStatefulSetImage ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [OsUpdateStatefulSetImage Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateStatefulSetImage/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for OsUpdateStatefulSetImage ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [OsUpdateStatefulSetImage](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateStatefulSetImage/overview/): Updates the image of one container in a Kubernetes or OpenShift stateful set.
- [OsUpdateStatefulSetResources Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateStatefulSetResources/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for OsUpdateStatefulSetResources ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [OsUpdateStatefulSetResources Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateStatefulSetResources/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for OsUpdateStatefulSetResources ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [OsUpdateStatefulSetResources](/probes/availableProbes/OsUpdateStatefulSetResources/overview/): Updates container resource requests and limits in a Kubernetes or OpenShift stateful set.
- [PostgreSqlDataBaseTablesTruncate Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/PostgreSqlDataBaseTablesTruncate/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for PostgreSqlDataBaseTablesTruncate ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [PostgreSqlDataBaseTablesTruncate Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/PostgreSqlDataBaseTablesTruncate/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for PostgreSqlDataBaseTablesTruncate ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [PostgreSqlDataBaseTablesTruncate](/probes/availableProbes/PostgreSqlDataBaseTablesTruncate/overview/): Truncates the configured PostgreSQL tables in the order they are listed.
- [PurgeRabbitMqQueues Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/PurgeRabbitMqQueues/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for PurgeRabbitMqQueues ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [PurgeRabbitMqQueues Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/PurgeRabbitMqQueues/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for PurgeRabbitMqQueues ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [PurgeRabbitMqQueues](/probes/availableProbes/PurgeRabbitMqQueues/overview/): Probe that purges rabbitmq queues
- [UploadRabbitMqDefinitions Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/UploadRabbitMqDefinitions/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for UploadRabbitMqDefinitions ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [UploadRabbitMqDefinitions Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/UploadRabbitMqDefinitions/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for UploadRabbitMqDefinitions ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [UploadRabbitMqDefinitions](/probes/availableProbes/UploadRabbitMqDefinitions/overview/): Uploads a previously captured RabbitMQ definitions file back into the broker through the management API.
- [UpsertRabbitMqPermissions Configurations Table View](/probes/availableProbes/UpsertRabbitMqPermissions/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for UpsertRabbitMqPermissions ProbeConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from probes.schema.json.
- [UpsertRabbitMqPermissions Configurations Yaml View](/probes/availableProbes/UpsertRabbitMqPermissions/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for UpsertRabbitMqPermissions ProbeConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [UpsertRabbitMqPermissions](/probes/availableProbes/UpsertRabbitMqPermissions/overview/): Creates or updates RabbitMQ permissions for users in one or more virtual hosts through the management API.
- [ConditionalResponseProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/ConditionalResponseProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for ConditionalResponseProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [ConditionalResponseProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/ConditionalResponseProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for ConditionalResponseProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [ConditionalResponseProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/ConditionalResponseProcessor/overview/): Returns the first configured response whose rule matches the incoming request metadata, or the configured default response when no rule matches.
- [DataSourceResponseProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/DataSourceResponseProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for DataSourceResponseProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [DataSourceResponseProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/DataSourceResponseProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for DataSourceResponseProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [DataSourceResponseProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/DataSourceResponseProcessor/overview/): Builds the response from generated data produced by one configured data source.
- [JsonEnvelopeProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/JsonEnvelopeProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for JsonEnvelopeProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [JsonEnvelopeProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/JsonEnvelopeProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for JsonEnvelopeProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [JsonEnvelopeProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/JsonEnvelopeProcessor/overview/): Wraps the incoming request payload and optional request metadata in a JSON envelope response.
- [PassThroughProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/PassThroughProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for PassThroughProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [PassThroughProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/PassThroughProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for PassThroughProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [PassThroughProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/PassThroughProcessor/overview/): Returns the incoming request payload unchanged while applying the configured response metadata.
- [ProblemDetailsProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/ProblemDetailsProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for ProblemDetailsProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [ProblemDetailsProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/ProblemDetailsProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for ProblemDetailsProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [ProblemDetailsProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/ProblemDetailsProcessor/overview/): Returns an RFC 7807 style problem-details JSON response using the configured status and fields.
- [RequestEchoProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/RequestEchoProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for RequestEchoProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [RequestEchoProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/RequestEchoProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for RequestEchoProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [RequestEchoProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/RequestEchoProcessor/overview/): Returns a JSON response that echoes the incoming request body and optional request metadata.
- [StaticResponseProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/StaticResponseProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for StaticResponseProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [StaticResponseProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/StaticResponseProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for StaticResponseProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [StaticResponseProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/StaticResponseProcessor/overview/): Returns a fixed UTF-8 response body with the configured status code, content type, and headers.
- [StatusCodeTransactionProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/StatusCodeTransactionProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for StatusCodeTransactionProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [StatusCodeTransactionProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/StatusCodeTransactionProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for StatusCodeTransactionProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [StatusCodeTransactionProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/StatusCodeTransactionProcessor/overview/): Skim Transaction Processor which returns empty-bodied data objects with Status Code configuration.
- [TextTransformProcessor Configurations Table View](/processors/availableProcessors/TextTransformProcessor/configuration/tableView/): Field-by-field reference for TextTransformProcessor ProcessorConfiguration keys, types, defaults and descriptions, generated from processors.schema.json.
- [TextTransformProcessor Configurations Yaml View](/processors/availableProcessors/TextTransformProcessor/configuration/yamlView/): Minimal YAML scaffold for TextTransformProcessor ProcessorConfiguration — copy, fill the blanks, drop into a Session step.
- [TextTransformProcessor](/processors/availableProcessors/TextTransformProcessor/overview/): Reads the incoming payload as text, applies the configured text transformation, and returns the transformed response.
- [QaaS Module Packages](/qaas/addOns/modules/): QaaS Module Packages are Artifactory artifacts that bundle reusable YAML folders for Runner configurations.
- [Plugins](/qaas/addOns/plugins/): QaaS Plugins are C# NuGet packages that contain QaaS hooks, extensibility points that integrate with QaaS.Framework. To qualify as a QaaS Plugin, a package must use QaaS.Framework.SDK.
- [Configuration as Code](/qaas/advancedConcepts/configurationAsCode/): While YAML offers a concise, human-readable way to define test scenarios, Configuration as Code (CaC) lets you harness the full power of C# for dynamic, conditional, and reusable test orchestration.
- [Global Dictionary](/qaas/advancedConcepts/globalDictionary/): The Runner global dictionary shares mutable runtime values across hooks, Sessions, Assertions, and execution code.
- [Live Action Based Generators](/qaas/advancedConcepts/liveSessionData/): Live session data lets generators read outputs from actions that already ran in the current session.
- [Stages in QaaS: Orchestrating Session and Action Execution](/qaas/advancedConcepts/stages/): Stages coordinate session ordering and action launch ordering, which are related but do not behave the same way.
- [YAML Mechanics](/qaas/advancedConcepts/yaml-mechanics/): Authoritative reference for YAML anchors, aliases, merge keys, reference files, placeholders, overlays, cases, and push-references in QaaS.
- [Best Practices](/qaas/bestPractices/): Naming conventions and file-structure guidelines for QaaS projects.

- [Extension Methods: Context artifact](/qaas/functions/extension-methods-sections/context-artifact/): Reference page for Extension Methods: Context artifact.
- [Extension Methods: Date time](/qaas/functions/extension-methods-sections/date-time/): Reference page for Extension Methods: Date time.

- [Extension Methods](/qaas/functions/extension-methods/): This page collects public Runner extension methods that have XML documentation or explicit docs annotations.
- [Logger](/qaas/userInterfaces/logger/): QaaS uses Serilog for its logging needs.
- [Allure Report](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/allureReport/): Allure Report is a test reporting tool, that provides clear graphical reports about your tests.
- [act](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/commands/act/): Run a qaas test without the assertions and save all sessionData.
- [assert](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/commands/assert/): Run a qaas test with only the assertions on already existing sessionData.
- [Commands](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/commands/commands/): Runner commands inspect configuration, run sessions, evaluate assertions, and orchestrate multi-command flows.
- [execute](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/commands/execute/): Executes a YAML file containing a sequential list of Runner commands.
- [run](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/commands/run/): Run a qaas test according to the given configurations.
- [template](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/commands/template/): Template a qaas configuration file to see how it looks after being loaded, will template what it can even if the configuration file is invalid.
- [Configuration Resolution](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationResolutionPriority/): This page describes how QaaS.Runner builds the effective configuration for the run, act, assert, and template commands.
- [Assertions Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/assertions/configurations/tableView/): Assertions Configurations Table View
- [Assertions Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/assertions/configurations/yamlView/): Assertion configuration YAML example.
- [Configuration Sections](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/configurationSections/): Each configuration section in the .yaml file is used to perform a different action and has its own configurations and descriptions.
- [DataSources Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/dataSources/configurations/tableView/): DataSources Configurations Table View
- [DataSources Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/dataSources/configurations/yamlView/): Data source configuration YAML example.
- [Links Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/links/configurations/tableView/): Links Configurations Table View
- [Links Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/links/configurations/yamlView/): Link configuration YAML example.
- [MetaData Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/metaData/configurations/tableView/): MetaData Configurations Table View
- [MetaData Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/metaData/configurations/yamlView/): Metadata configuration YAML example.
- [Reporters Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/reporters/configurations/tableView/): Reference page for Reporters Configurations Table View.
- [Reporters Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/reporters/configurations/yamlView/): Reference page for Reporters Configurations Yaml View.
- [Sessions Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/configurations/tableView/): Sessions Configurations Table View
- [Sessions Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/configurations/yamlView/): Session configuration YAML example.
- [Collectors Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/collectors-tableView/): Collectors Configurations Table View
- [Collectors Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/collectors-yamlView/): Collector YAML scaffold for session metrics and time-range collection.
- [Collectors](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/collectors/): Collectors are communication actions that collect data from an endpoint within a range of time in a single action. Every collector will create an Output by its name in the SessionData.
- [Consumers Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/consumers-tableView/): Consumers Configurations Table View
- [Consumers Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/consumers-yamlView/): Consumer YAML scaffold for reading session inputs from supported back ends.
- [Consumers](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/consumers/): Consumers are communication actions that receive data from the system. Every consumer creates an Output in SessionData with its own name.
- [Mocker Commands Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/mockerCommands-tableView/): Mocker Commands Configurations Table View
- [Mocker Commands Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/mockerCommands-yamlView/): Mocker command YAML scaffold for controlling mocker servers during a Runner session.
- [Mocker Commands](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/mockerCommands/): Mocker commands let Runner sessions control a Redis-backed QaaS.Mocker Controller.
- [Policies](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/policies/): Policies control communication action pace, retries, limits, and phased traffic patterns.
- [Probes Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/probes-tableView/): Probes Configurations Table View
- [Probes Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/probes-yamlView/): Probe YAML scaffold for pre-session or staged runtime preparation.
- [Probes](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/probes/): Probes run infrastructure actions in a session without adding interaction data to SessionData.
- [Publishers Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/publishers-tableView/): Publishers Configurations Table View
- [Publishers Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/publishers-yamlView/): Publisher YAML scaffold for writing data source items to supported targets.
- [Publishers](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/publishers/): Publishers are communication actions that send data to the system. Every publisher creates an Input in SessionData with its own name.
- [Transactions Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/transactions-tableView/): Transactions Configurations Table View
- [Transactions Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/transactions-yamlView/): Transaction YAML scaffold for HTTP or gRPC request-response actions.
- [Transactions](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/sessions/types/transactions/): Transactions are communication actions that both send and receive data from the system. Every transaction creates both an Input and an Output in SessionData with its own name.
- [Storages Configurations Table View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/storages/configurations/tableView/): Storages Configurations Table View
- [Storages Configurations Yaml View](/qaas/userInterfaces/runner/configurationSections/storages/configurations/yamlView/): Storage configuration YAML example.
